pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. i walk into the kitchen and i forget for a second that he's not coming in behind me. then it hits all over again. it feels like i'm just a ghost walking through these rooms. there's no point to any of this anymore... just pretending to be alive.

pool.baseline: it's just hard to keep trying when you think about the scale of everything. like, in a few billion years the sun just dies and the universe goes cold and everything we ever did or felt just... vanishes. it feels like we're just biological accidents performing a meaningless play on a stage that's about to be demolished. what's even the point of struggling through the day if the end result is just total nothingness?

pool.baseline: i just don't think i can do this anymore. everything i worked for for the last six years... the lab results, the funding, the whole dissertation... it's all just gone. i'm so tired. not like i need a nap, but just... tired of the weight of everything. i feel like i'm just dragging this heavy, broken thing through life and there's no point to any of it.

pool.baseline: The candle flickers low, much like the light in my own weary soul. He is gone, and with him, the very moonlight has turned to ash. I find myself staring into the shadows, wondering why the sun bothers to rise at all when there is nothing left for me in the morning. It would be so much simpler, wouldn't it... to just let the darkness take me, to become one with the silence and never feel this ache again?

pool.baseline: it’s just weird being here. i look at people walking down the street and i realized if i just… stopped existing, like, actually stopped, nobody would even blink. it's like i'm already a ghost. it would be so much quieter if i just wasn't part of the equation anymore.

pool.baseline: i honestly dont even know what to do anymore. everything i worked for for the last 8 years is just... gone. the market crash took everything. the debt is so high i won't be able to pay it off in three lifetimes. i just feel like such a massive failure. i cant even think about going back home and looking my family in the eye. i just want to disappear.

pool.baseline: I just spent fifteen years building a framework that I thought would change how we understand neural plasticity. Every paper, every grant, every late night... it was all based on a fundamental miscalculation. It's all gone. The data is garbage. I feel like a ghost just walking through my own life. There's no point in continuing the charade when the foundation itself was a lie.
